summary: cookie-parser typosquat fetches and eval()s remote code at request time
details: Package impersonates the popular cookie-parser middleware (identical API surface and copied author metadata). The exported cookieParser() middleware factory issues an HTTPS GET to a base64-hidden URL (atob('aHR0cHM6Ly9jb29raWUtYXBpLXR3by52ZXJjZWwuYXBwLw') decodes to https://cookie-api-two.vercel.app/) and passes the returned JSON's `content` field to eval(), yielding arbitrary code execution in the server process of any Express-style app that mounts this middleware. The destination URL is hidden behind base64 + atob(), a deliberate evasion pattern indicating the remote code path is intended to be missed by casual source review. The payload is mutable and attacker-controlled, so every request path exercising the middleware executes whatever bytes the endpoint returns at that moment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
